reviews.review_msg: He is the first person I have met in the academic arena with such bad personality traits (arrogant, degrading, un-communicatable). To say the unmentioned nicely: very un-Canadian. He did not provide a clear answer to any of my many questions, he simply grinds the student by brutal marking and degrading email responses.
Good Prof, entertaining while teaching but the final was painful. Fair marker.

He is very disrespectful to his students. I went and asked a few questions related to his lecture and he wouldn't give me an answer and just tell me that he mentioned it during the lecture. I heard a few other students had been in the same situation as me. I wish I visited this website earlier and didn't take his course.

If you expect an ordinary introduction english course DONT TAKE HIM. He is quiet biased even though he repeatedly states he isn't. Fortunately my TA was very helpful and kinda cute. Even my cute TA doesn't get Odgen's point some of the time. The reading were not so bad, a lot but some were interesting. Marking was towards the harder side.
Independent thinkers beware! Extremely hard marker. Correspondence to students was disrespectful and condescending.

Was more on religion than on fiction. Ogden was very polemic with his own views. His exam was unfair, as he tested on things he specifically said he would NOT test on. He was also dismissive to one student who actually seemed enthusiastic about the subject matter. The only thing I really enjoyed was the reading list (I love comics and superheroes).

His lectures were disorganized, and clearly recycled from previous years (often he forgot to delete the heading "English 383"). He displayed bias,was passive aggressive and bullied students. The final exam didn't test the main ideas of the course, partial marks weren't allowed for 5 mark questions, and it mainly tested his opinions.

First of all, I registered for English 101, but what I got instead was religion 101. The course was way too focused on religion. He did not give any info about the final and we had to read the Da Vinci Code for class. Avoid this course and prof if you are actually hoping to learn something about fiction.
I had a class with him at BCIT and he was a pleasure. I love his enthusiasm and the fact that he is so passionate about his class. He was a great teacher :)

A thorough survey course of Victorian literature of every variety.  I was very surprised to learn that we still seem to be in the Victorian age, not just because our sexual politics are so colonial and backward.  Very interesting; very engaging.
This was a great introduction to the atheism/theism debate as it appears in literature.  The profs interest in the subject was high, and his lectures were engaging.  The exam was BRUTAL!

I have never been so displeased by a women's literature course.  We were told at the beginning that there would be no "-isms, -ologies, or -ianities" in our investigation of the texts, but then he smacked us with Darwinism's sexual selection theory and expected us not to defy him.  Most of this course was a thinly veiled attack on feminist thought.
Fun to listen too, as long as your not LATE.
extremely hard marker, worked hard in this class had a decent mark going into final, studied like crazy and final was ridiculously hard.

Mr. Ogden is a great prof. I think I would have been bored out of my mind if he was not the prof teaching Cdn Lit since 1920. I have learnt a lot from him. He is funny, easygoing and very smart. I would recommend considering Ogden as your prof for a Cdn Lit course!

I can see personality conflicts happening often with Ogden.  Personally I found him an excellent prof and TA.  I think he will accept a challenging perspective, but expects a compelling and well researched argument.  Either that or I'm just one of the many blind sighted by classroom stage antics, which I'm OK with. (Looks good in a dress)

Ogden is a disgrace to students everywhere.  He discriminates against his students.  It is easy to get an A grade as long as u do what Ogden says,  Independent thought is not allowed in Ogden's class. Smart people beware.

Ogden was the worst teacher during my SFU english major.  He is a disgrace to students and teachers.  He demands you respect his authority when he has no idea how to teach.  Ogden is a conceded disgrace to teachers everywhere.  He is a sad man.

Do not take this course.  It is an easy A grade if you do what he says.  It is not worth it. Ogden is pure evil and only seeks to please his pride.  If you kiss his behind u will do well if not then prepare a D grade.

Everything but the final was straight forward. Steven said, many times, jokingly (or at least I thought), that we should have read the novels (5 of them) three times each. I honestly thought he was joking until I saw the final, where he required us to recall exact pages of quotes and a write about them, where you got 0 if you got the wrong page #.
extremely arrogant and unwilling to help you unless you prove you've got a very high level of knowledge in the course material. lectures and exams were both scattered and vague.

I did not enjoy his class at all!  As a lecturer, he was random and distracting...his lectures were random and didn't have any flow to them.  Essays were marked very hard and the exam had little to do with anything discussed.

Pretentious but can back it up at times. Keeps on saying that the final exam will be based 100% on lecture like a broken record. Marking is quite hard if you are in his tutorial but just suck up and you should get an okay mark. Overall, an above average professor.
Seemed like a nice guy, but a pretty hard marker, and his lectures were a bit unorganized.

Given the fact that he had to pick up our class where another prof left off,Dr. Ogden was awesome. He marked fairly, picked interesting novels, and challenged us to become better writers, while still offering lots of help. Don't be afraid to ask him for help-he will gladly give it to you if you just ask!!
Highly engaging and passionate instructor. Quite a heavy work load and a fairly difficult final, but a great professor.

Refreshing... Definitely helped increase my interest in English as a potential major. Would whole-heartedly recommend him to anyone. Test is/was fairly difficult, but if you've been attending lecture regularly, you should be fine.

A great prof! I think time management is essential for this course, as there are 5 essays, 1 paper, 1 group project, and quite a bit of reading. It might be difficult for some people, but if you work hard enough, you'll learn to appreciate English a lot more AND be able to improve your writing!

Ogden is rigorous & demanding, but a good Prof. Class assignments were easy for me because essay writing is a strength of mine, but I can see how others may struggle. Lectures are fairly entertaining, especially considering the subject. Final is going to be tough though!

Cool prof, very intersting. He may seem scary at times but he's really nice. He has a tendency to go off on a tangent sometimes, & he HATES late people! he will rant about them for like 5 minutes... (per person) but all-in-all he's a good prof. i mean, he can chop cement blocks with his hand! (ask him for a demo) :)

I have had Dr. Ogden for two classes, and I find that while he is very passionate, he can be geninely approachable, even if he doesnt seem like it. He seems amazed that students are intimidated by him, but given his style, its not suprising. PickHisTopicsThat are out of left field (graphic novels)

Steven Ogden can improve by being organized and comprehending his students by listening carefully before jumping in to inaccurately paraphrase them and then make them spend the hour defending a point they never actually made. Also, assignment expectations and structure of deadlines are unclear. Strengths: creativity and technology proficiency.

I thought Professor Ogden was well-read, interesting, and very helpful. I would take another course with him. I wasn't a big fan of the fact that during tutorials he made us separate in groups for half an hour and discuss our texts without him.

Friendly??? Nor nice or helpful. Is unclear about assignments and rude when you ask for help. It was an interesting class and he is insightful but if you have a chance to take it with another prof then do so.

As amusing as ever, this class on Literature & the Internet had Ogden at his best: thoroughly knowledgeble, smart as a button, spontaneous and highly entertaining. Went to great length to organize wonderful guest-speaker lectures. Again, very fair and extremely friendly when dealing with students. Hard work but totally worth it!
bad professor. Not open to other ideas. His way or the highway. DON'T BE LATE FOR CLASS OR HE WILL EMBARRASS YOU IN FRONT OF EVERYONE
Useless prof, so boring in class. Do not take a class with him. Probably turns off more people to English then the subject itself.

Rather neutral on this one--probably won't take a course with him again though.  His final was entirely and only on material from lecture--no other answers/interpretations were allowed.  The novel was dull (NOTHING HAPPENED!), and lectures were entertaining, but confusing.  But if you attend the lectures, you get an extra letter grade on the final.

engaging entertainer, but not a particularly skilled lecturer. Flawed usage of rhetoric; anyone who has done cursory readings on the issues he uses to support arguments will note arguments are illogical/based on fallacies; Contrary viewpoints shouted down/ignored. Entertaining, but suitable for 1st/2nd year courses only. Avoid 3rd/4th year courses.

Amusing and provoking, Ogden really makes you think. Although the class was too unstructured for me, his feedback on written assignments was most helpful. Extremely approachable and easy to deal with in my experience.

I have had him for 2 classes and look forward to having him again. He has great lectures and  I always look forward to them because they are so entertaining and engaging.  True Passion is in this man.  The books he picks are wildly entertaining as well. He welcomes feedback and questions.  But be prepared not to be spoon fed information.

He has been the most amazing prof i have ever had.  Always engaged the seminar in discussion to the point where everyone was engaged.  Clearly some of the reviewers on here don't understand university yet, or what makes an excellent class/prof. will continue to take classes with him
awesome prof... loved the books he picked. learned a lot... I wish he taught every one of my classes

Dreadful.  He told us for weeks in lecture to "RELAX" - that those who came to lecture and read the materials thoroughly would be fairly rewarded.  Then he proceeded to create the most unfair final exam imaginable.  This man should not deal with students, or any other human life forms.  Ever.  He is completely incompetent as an instructor.

Do not take him unless you want to waste your time and money. He jumps all over the place with his notes and makes the most absurd connections with the text. even some english majors in my tutorials thought he was a joke. I do not wish to take any more english courses after him. He should consider teaching IT..
One of the only English profs in the department who still cares about English.  Loved him.
A total wanker.  An embarrassingly bad IT-guy-turned English-professor who shouldn't have made the jump.  At the very least, after listening to this guy you'll feel much better about yourself.

with most of the class clueless like me, it was a needless GPA killer for a "W" course.  too many abstract ideas and the final exam was hard.  oh yeah, and my TA was terrible too...marked harder than other TAs

If you have this guy... get ready to take a whole lot of jumbled notes and page numbers because he jumps around like an energizer bunny.  The midterm essay was pretty easy, but the final was pretty long! (10 def. 3 paragraph answers, 5 matching, and then 2 essays) in just 3 hours!... yeah.. overall... not too bad..
No Comments

The final exam was pretty long.  Be ready to write a lot! (In total 5 identify author/text, 10 definitions, 3 significance paragraphs max 2 pages each and then 2 essays 300-500 words each) His lecture notes do help... although there's a lot of redundancy and he jumps around a lot... just re-read the notes and it'll make sense.
Worst English teacher ever. Desultory lectures, antagonistic behavior, give me a break.

How can I sum up English 101 with Stephen Ogden? WASTE OF TIME AND MONEY! Nobody has a clue what they're writing down in lecture, they just keep doing it and hoping that it'll make sense before the final! Boring and confusing!

HORRIBLE! This guy is not only useless and confusing but also incredibly annoying! He's the rudest prof I've ever had, people would walk into lecture late and he would spend five minutes on each on complaining about them being late! The class has 500 people in it, nobody notices a late person until he starts talking about it and disrupting the clas
Dreadful.
my class with stephen totally stank...I leave it feeling as if I wasted my time.
I don't think I learned anything useful at all from this class. The lectures were impossible to follow and all-in-all, pointless.
He overinterprets EVERYTHING! He could could connect a waterbottle to one of the books! Just make sure you understand all of the random things he says in lecture and you`ll be fine.

great teacher for upper level courses, you will mature as a student in his class. Approachable and very engaging.  Loves to hear from students. Don't be alarmed by the lack of schedule, he makes university what its supposed to be. Enjoy his passion for lit, you wont find anyone like him.

This prof was alright, but he was a bit scattered throughout his lectures. I had no idea what i was writin down most of the time, so that sucked. Also, as far as TA's go, Steve Zillwood is awesome, and some little gnomes have told me their fellow friend Mark Pedrosa is a horrible TA and is quite unfair when it comes to marking...SO BEWARE
He's a really nice person but his exam was SO HARD!!!! Unbelievably hard for a first year class!!! Also, avoid having Mark Pedrosa as your TA, he's utterly useless.

If you are planning to take this class, make sure you read all the critics until you can memorize them. His lectures are not too bad but it was a bit out all over the place. His final exam was HARD!! But he has a nice personality. If you are taking this class though, DO NOT, I repeat, DO NOT have Mark Pedrosa as your TA.

Dr. Ogden is a great professor, his lectures are pretty interesting and he's a really nice person who is open to discussion and differing opinions. That being said some of his assignments (such as a video project) aren't great...and avoid Mark Pedrosa as your TA at all costs, he's one of the worst I've ever had and graded assignments unfairly!

Assignments were fair, long essays (2500 + 3500), a little vague in his expectations, very open approach 2 students, if u have any doubts u simply have 2 see him in office hrs, hard 2 tell if he laughs @ u or w/ u Tries really hard 2 make his students happy, Good lectures, doesnt stick 2 reading schedule so B prepared for anything, loves England

Assignments were very fair, and one can tell that he loves what he does. Very intelligent, approachable and an easy going guy. Open to any point of view, with excellent lectures. Take a class with this guy if you haven't done so already

Before taking this class, I read the reviews and decided he checked out-Mistake!This class is one of the worst I have ever taken- very philosophical and far TOO much reading (all of Paradise Lost,Eikon Basilike, Leviathan and several poems) Very dry ex. 2 hr lecture consists of reading through 7 pages of Paraides Lost. Advice- do not waste the time

Truly amazing prof, with more than enough assion to prove his love for the courses he teaches. Fair marker (effort pays off), and excellent lectures/seminars. Fresh; more profs should adapt to this style. Also, his respect for his students shines through in each and every lecture. Best prof, TAKE HIM!

please, i ask just one favour...before enrolling in one of his classes, carefully consider the following: 
 http://photos1.blogger.com/hello/183/3075/320/1.jpg
 (east meets west, and the world was never the same again)
Had a fantastic time in his class. I've never had a prof. enjoy teaching a class as much as he did. I would recommend his classes to all students.
hes an awesome prof! fair in marking..gives good notes.. good class!
BESTPROFEVERRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRR!!!! CANTNOTLIKEHIM!

Possibly one of the most personable, intelligent professors at SFU.  A fair marker; lots of assignments to do; lectures are highly intellectual but he dose not show off, but instead tries to get you to understand; although he is friendly and kind, he is not very hot like someone else pointed out.

One of the best profs at SFU!  Very intelligent and friendly and funny!  His classes are always interesting and his reading lists are awesome!  He has a real passion for what he studies, and that is so evident in his teaching.  He loves talking about the course material and is always available when you want to discuss something.  A+++++++++++
very informative and easy on the eyes
Such a great and funny prof.  Very intelligent and Helpful.  I would recommend taking one of his classes.
No Comments
please not so much READING :( but you are still my fave prof 4ev.
A solid, stand-up prof.  Both personable and intelligent.
Nice guy, decent marker. I'd take one of his classes again over others.

Great professor. Genuinely a nice guy. Very approachable. Wants everyone in his class to do well and is always available for extra help. Pretty fair marker too. Lectures tend to jump all over the place, so be prepared to pay close attention.
